articles.title: Bayern Munich retain Bundesliga title after beating Stuttgart
articles.description: Bayern Munich retained their Bundesliga title after a 4-2 win at home to VfB Stuttgart on Sunday as they ticked off the second leg of a possible sextuple. Vincent Kompany's men wrapped up the championship with four games to spare having extended their lead over Borussia Dortmund to an unassailable 15 points.

articles.title: Bayern parrot titles with cockatoo T-shirts as players hail mentality
articles.description: Bayern Munich stars said a bulked-up mentality was key to retaining the Bundesliga title with four games to spare, while also destroying the league season scoring record by netting 109 so far. A 4-2 home win over VfB Stuttgart on Sunday sealed a second straight crown, with just one game lost in the league this term.

articles.title: Dominant Bayern retain Bundesliga title after beating Stuttgart
articles.description: Bayern Munich retained their Bundesliga title after a 4-2 win at home to VfB Stuttgart on Sunday as they ticked off the second leg of a possible sextuple. articles.title: Calm Kompany helps Bayern prosper as Kane and Olise shine
articles.description: Vincent Kompany was nowhere near Bayern Munich's first choice after Thomas Tuchel's fractious exit in 2024. articles.title: Rybakina beats Muchova to claim Stuttgart Open title
articles.description: Australian Open champion Elena Rybakina claimed a second title at the Stuttgart Open with a win against Karolina Muchova on Sunday. The Kazakhstani defeated Muchova 7-5, 6-1 in 1 hour and 18 minutes to claim her 13th WTA title.

articles.title: Siddle holds firm as Multan beat Kings in PSL
articles.description: Multan Sultans held firm to beat Karachi Kings by 11 runs to move up to second in the Pakistan Super League table. Multan appeared in control of the match after reducing Kings to 151-6 after 16 overs as they chased 208. Veteran Australian seamer Peter Siddle, 41, dismissed Abbas for 34 to start the 19th over, however, and conceded only five runs.

articles.title: Ipswich rally to hold Middlesbrough via Jack Clarke’s controversial penalty
articles.description: Jack Clarke fired home an 87th-minute penalty to save a point for Ipswich in a pulsating 2-2 draw with promotion rivals Middlesbrough. Jarred Gillett awarded the spot-kick when he adjudged Adilson Malanda had tugged the substitute George Hirst in the box. Middlesbrough, who are on a seven-match winless run, went ahead through David Strelec only for Kasey McAteer to equalise five minutes later. The visitors took the lead through Tommy Conway but Clarke’s spot-kick saw the points shared. Continue reading...

articles.title: UAAP: Men's final Four race tightens as UST wins, NU falters
articles.description: SCHEDULE: UAAP Season 88 volleyball tournament – second round MANILA, Philippines—The Final Four race in the UAAP Season 88 men’s volleyball tournament intensified on Sunday following key wins by University of Santo Tomas and La Salle. The Golden Spikers stayed in the hunt for the twice-to-beat playoff incentive after defeating University of the Philippines, 25-14, 25-17, 25-16, at Mall of Asia Arena. Josh Ybañez led UST anew with an all-around effort of 17 points, eight receptions and three digs to power the Golden Spikers to a 9-4 record. Treyvor Valera helped the cause with 11 points built on eight attacks [...]... Keep on reading: UAAP: Men's final Four race tightens as UST wins, NU falters
